sanskrit: दिवि सूर्यसहस्रस्य भवेद्युगपदुत्थिता। यदि भाः सदृशी सा स्याद्भासस्तस्य महात्मनः॥
transliteration: divi sūrya-sahasrasya bhaved yugapad utthitā | yadi bhāḥ sadṛśī sā syād bhāsas tasya mahātmanaḥ ||
literal_meaning: If the light of a thousand suns were to blaze forth all at once in the sky, that splendor might resemble the radiance of that great-souled Being.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s mind is overwhelmed by a transfinite vision that shatters ordinary perceptual limits; the psyche registers the Absolute as an unbearable surplus of brilliance, forcing a re-frame of identity and reality.
psychological_state: Acute numinous experience producing ego-boundary rupture and transient dissociation; a peak/trauma overlap that necessitates immediate psychic re-integration.
trigger_scenario: A war-veteran turned astronaut sees Earth from space for the first time and is flooded by a light so intense it feels like staring into a thousand suns—his worldview, self-story, and moral certainties collapse in a single moment.
anchor_text: Let overwhelming light dissolve the small self into boundless being.
